canonical_definition: The process of generating a structured, graph-based representation of a natural language query, which can then be used to search a knowledge graph.
papers: MiCRo: Mixture Modeling and Context-aware Routing for Personalized Preference Learning
Social Bias in Multilingual Language Models: A Survey

definitions.text: The process of generating a structured, graph-based representation of a natural language query, which can then be used to search a knowledge graph.
definitions.paper: MiCRo: Mixture Modeling and Context-aware Routing for Personalized Preference Learning

definitions.text: Producing executable graph database queries (e.g., in openCypher) from natural language questions to retrieve structured data from a knowledge graph.
definitions.paper: Social Bias in Multilingual Language Models: A Survey

characterization: The process of generating a structured, graph-based representation of a natural language query, which can then be used to search a knowledge graph.
